reasoning: The Lebanese government newly formed in early 2025 has just begun efforts to seek a new IMF program after failing to fulfill the demands of the 2022 staff-level agreement. As of late April 2025, Economy Minister Amer Bisat indicated that Lebanon hopes to sign a preliminary staff-level agreement 'within months', citing 'late summer' (which falls after the July 1 deadline) as a target. Furthermore, a staff-level agreement is only preliminary; formal IMF Executive Board approval is required for the IMF to actually agree to provide money (loans or grants). Given that even the preliminary step is targeted for late summer, there is virtually no chance of finalizing a full funding agreement before July 1, 2025. No evidence suggests an expedited emergency financing instrument is in the works either. Therefore, the probability is assessed at the floor value of 5%.
